recurringPattern: Multi-column footer with logo/description in first column, 3-4 link columns (Product, Resources, Company, Legal), social media icon row, and a copyright bar at the bottom with a thin separator line.
designHarm: The four-column footer with link-heavy structure assumes every site needs the same footer density. Users almost never scan footer links — the pattern wastes vertical space and adds cognitive load for accessibility (screen readers must navigate all links). The copyright bar is often taller than necessary for legal text.
legitimateExceptions: Large corporate sites with legal mandate to list many links
Documentation sites where footer serves as secondary navigation
possibleFixtures: Minimal footer with only copyright and essential legal links
originalObservations: AI-generated footers always default to exactly 4 columns regardless of actual link count.
Column headings are always Product, Resources, Company, Legal, or close variants.
The social media row uses circular icon buttons with identical sizing.
